text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals—Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal might be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." Breaking the project into milestones helps track progress, such as completing a design phase or finishing a prototype. Each milestone should produce specific deliverables, like a report or a software feature. Before starting, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, including team members and clients. This reduces misunderstandings and keeps the project on track toward its final objective.
